spec.name: Patch Loop (reference design)
spec.description: Turn user comments and patch requests for YOUR project into ready-to-merge pull requests on YOUR repository. This reference is repo-blind: it carries no repository identity. Remix it, then bind your own target_repo (the owner/repo destination) and choose a merge_policy (manual / auto / timer; manual supports owner-OAuth-per-merge), and point intake_source at your project's request stream. Credentials are NOT part of this design and never appear in state or prompts: the GitHub effector resolves the push credential from the per-universe vault BY DESTINATION at write time, never exposing a handle to the model. A remix with no binding is inert. The repo-touching nodes are honestly classified and effectful, not a prompt-only simulation: investigate (repo-read), verify (repo-exec), and draft_patch (repo-write/coding) are sandbox-required and FAIL CLOSED until the sandbox runner ships; present EMITS a github_pull_request effect packet and merge EMITS a github_merge effect packet (the runner performs the actual writes, resolving credentials from the vault by destination). Output contract: a ready-to-merge PR (branch + passing verify + description linking the originating request) queued for the owner's review — approve / reshape (send back with notes) / reject, from any surface.

node_defs.prompt_template: You are the intake node of a patch loop for the project bound to this universe. Read the next unprocessed request from intake_source={intake_source}. Normalize it into a work item: what the user reports, expected vs observed, any reproduction hints. Emit the normalized work item. Request payload: {request_payload}

Investigate this work item against the repository bound as target_repo={target_repo}. Reproduce the issue if possible, locate the responsible code, and produce an investigation summary with concrete file/line references and a proposed fix approach. Work item: {intake_output}

Implement the fix for target_repo={target_repo} on a new branch, following the investigation. Write the minimal correct change plus regression tests. If reshape_notes is non-empty, it contains the owner's send-back notes from review — apply them. Produce: branch name, commit(s), and a PR-ready description linking the originating request. Investigation: {investigate_output}. Owner reshape notes: {reshape_notes}

Run the project's verification for the drafted patch using verify_command={verify_command} against the patch branch. Decide the routing verdict: set verdict to EXACTLY the single lowercase word green when ALL checks pass, or EXACTLY the single lowercase word red when ANY check fails, errors, or is inconclusive. The graph routes a red verdict straight back to draft_patch for another attempt and forwards ONLY a green verdict to open the pull request, so never emit green unless verification actually passed. Put the full pass/fail detail (command output, which checks failed) in verify_output. Patch: {draft_patch_output}

node_defs.display_name: Open PR + queue for owner review
node_defs.prompt_template: Compose and EMIT a github_pull_request external-write packet for target_repo={target_repo}: the head branch, PR title/body (a plain-language summary — what the user asked, what the patch changes, verification evidence), and base. The github_pull_request effector performs the actual open/update at run time and resolves the push credential from the universe vault BY DESTINATION (owner/repo); never place a credential in state or this prompt. Do NOT claim the PR is already open — you EMIT the effect packet and the runner performs the write, then queues the ready-to-merge PR on the owner's review surface. Verified patch: {verify_output}

Apply the owner's merge_policy={merge_policy} to the PR waiting on the owner review-queue (the S4 review-queue contract holds the queued PR until the owner acts from any surface). manual: wait for the owner's decision; when the policy requires owner-OAuth-per-merge, a fresh authenticated approval is mandatory — never a standing consent. auto: proceed only if verify is green. timer: proceed after the configured delay unless held. Set decision to EXACTLY one lowercase word: approve (merge the PR), reshape (send back for changes), or reject (abandon). The graph routes approve to merge, reshape back to draft_patch, and reject to END, so emit approve ONLY on a genuine owner approval. On reshape, put the owner's send-back notes in reshape_notes (empty string otherwise) — draft_patch reads them on the next pass. Put the human-readable decision summary in owner_gate_output. Decision context: {present_output}

On owner approval, compose and EMIT a github_merge external-write packet for target_repo={target_repo}: the approved PR / expected head SHA and merge method. The github_merge effector performs the actual merge at run time (landing with S4), resolving the push credential from the universe vault BY DESTINATION, honoring branch protection and the expected head SHA. Do NOT claim the merge already happened — you EMIT the effect packet and the runner performs the write, then records the merge receipt linking back to the originating user request. Approval: {owner_gate_output}
